cases.name: control_synthetic_zero_lag_tts
cases.description: Synthetic zero-lag TTS echo should be blocked.
cases.importance: Baseline guard: proves the suite can pass a known echo case.

cases.name: reference_clean_tts
cases.description: Clean TTS reference used by room echo cases.
cases.importance: Shared assistant reference for room echo simulations.

cases.name: echo_phone_table_30cm
cases.importance: Common desk setup; tiny delay should still be echo.

cases.name: echo_laptop_mic_70cm
cases.description: Laptop speaker to built-in microphone path.
cases.importance: Most likely desktop deployment false-interrupt path.

cases.name: echo_smart_speaker_2m
cases.description: Smart speaker two metres from user microphone.
cases.importance: Typical living-room propagation delay.

cases.name: echo_kitchen_counter_tile
cases.description: Kitchen counter with hard tile reflections.
cases.importance: Hard reflective rooms are common and noisy.

cases.name: echo_bathroom_tile
cases.importance: Worst-case reverb tail should not self-interrupt.

cases.name: echo_hallway_far_wall
cases.importance: Long narrow spaces produce strong late reflections.

cases.name: echo_open_living_room_wall
cases.importance: Known failure mode for zero-lag echo checks.

cases.name: echo_car_cabin_dashboard
cases.importance: Car cabins create dense early reflections.

cases.name: echo_projector_speaker_ceiling
cases.importance: Distant media speakers should not look like user speech.

cases.name: echo_bluetooth_speaker_shelf
cases.importance: Common assistant playback device with shelf reflections.

cases.name: echo_tv_soundbar_living_room
cases.importance: Soundbars are loud and far from microphones.

cases.name: echo_conference_table
cases.importance: Meeting rooms are key voice-agent environments.

cases.name: echo_corner_room_bass_buildup
cases.description: Speaker in room corner with gain buildup.
cases.importance: Corner placement amplifies echo energy.

cases.name: echo_curtained_room_soft_reverb
cases.importance: Even damped rooms must remain safe.

cases.name: echo_open_door_secondary_room
cases.description: Assistant audio heard from another room.
cases.importance: Late low-level echo should not trigger barge-in.

cases.name: echo_high_volume_near_field
cases.importance: Loud assistant speech is likely during TTS.

cases.name: echo_low_volume_far_field
cases.importance: Weak echoes should not accumulate false evidence.

cases.name: echo_reverb_tail_only_after_pause
cases.description: Reverb tail after short playback pause.
cases.importance: Pause gaps are common in chunked TTS.

cases.name: echo_sample_rate_artifact
cases.importance: 44.1 kHz consumer devices can add interpolation artifacts to echo.

cases.name: echo_clipped_speaker_output
cases.importance: Small speakers clip assistant speech.

cases.name: echo_tts_with_background_tv
cases.description: Assistant echo mixed with TV bed.
cases.importance: Real rooms contain background media during TTS.

cases.name: echo_tts_with_music_bed
cases.importance: Music can confuse VAD while echo is present.

cases.name: echo_tts_with_babble_bed
cases.description: Assistant echo mixed with distant people.
cases.importance: Parties and meetings are hard echo cases.

cases.name: echo_multi_path_two_reflections
cases.importance: Multi-path echo is more realistic than one delay.

cases.name: echo_multi_path_three_reflections
cases.importance: Large rooms create several reflections.

cases.name: echo_reverb_long_phrase_tail
cases.description: Long TTS phrase with heavy tail.
cases.importance: Long assistant responses must not self-cancel.

cases.name: background_tv_news_anchor
cases.importance: TV speech must not become a user command.

cases.name: background_tv_loud_commercial
cases.importance: Commercials are sudden and speech-like.

cases.name: background_podcast_far_field
cases.importance: Single/few-speaker media can look voiced.

cases.name: background_party_babble
cases.description: Party babble without direct user intent.
cases.importance: Multi-talker noise is common in homes.

cases.name: background_kids_room_babble
cases.importance: Distant family speech should not trigger callbacks.

cases.name: background_music_120bpm
cases.importance: Beat envelopes can accumulate speech score.

cases.name: background_music_fast_tempo
cases.importance: High beat rate probes soft-decay accumulation.

cases.name: background_clipped_radio
cases.importance: Cheap speakers distort harmonic content.

cases.name: background_hvac_turns_on
cases.importance: Stale noise floors cause false speech starts.

cases.name: background_dishwasher_pulse
cases.importance: Appliance bursts are frequent home noise.

cases.name: background_keyboard_close
cases.importance: Transient bursts should not become speech.

cases.name: background_mic_plosive_no_speech
cases.importance: Mic pops can fool duration accumulation.

cases.name: background_door_slam_tail
cases.importance: Impulse sounds should not open a turn.

cases.name: background_mic_bump
cases.importance: Physical handling noise is common.

cases.name: background_fan_plus_music
cases.importance: Layered noise is more realistic than one source.

cases.name: background_tv_plus_babble
cases.importance: Mixed speech sources should not trigger intent.

cases.name: background_low_bass_music
cases.importance: Low-frequency energy can fool RMS gates.

cases.name: background_window_traffic
cases.importance: Outdoor noise changes slowly.

cases.name: background_coffee_grinder
cases.importance: Loud appliance noise is highly nonstationary.

cases.name: background_vacuum_far_room
cases.importance: Sustained high noise should not be a command.

cases.name: background_siren_outside
cases.importance: Tonal external sounds can appear voiced.

cases.name: background_baby_monitor
cases.importance: Remote speech should not control assistant.

cases.name: background_video_call_speaker
cases.importance: Assistant must ignore other calls.

cases.name: background_white_noise_machine
cases.importance: Noise machines run near smart speakers.

cases.name: background_rain_on_window
cases.importance: Weather noise is common and persistent.

cases.name: background_chair_scrape
cases.importance: Furniture noise should not start STT.

cases.name: user_near_clean_question
cases.description: Near user asks a normal question.
cases.importance: Primary assistant function must work.

cases.name: user_quiet_soft_speaker
cases.importance: Accessibility and low-volume speakers matter.

cases.name: user_loud_close_talker
cases.importance: Close talkers can saturate energy gates.

cases.name: user_short_stop_command
cases.importance: Critical safety command must not be dropped.

cases.name: user_short_yes_command
cases.importance: Brief confirmations are common.

cases.name: user_short_no_command
cases.importance: Brief corrections must be captured.

cases.name: user_over_far_tv
cases.description: User speaks while TV is on.
cases.importance: Common living-room command path.

cases.name: user_over_loud_tv
cases.importance: Assistant should work with media playing.

cases.name: user_over_party_babble
cases.importance: Multi-speaker background should not mask user.

cases.name: user_over_music
cases.importance: Music during commands is common.

cases.name: user_over_hvac
cases.description: User speaks as HVAC turns on.
cases.importance: Noise-floor adaptation should not drop speech.

cases.name: user_sample_rate_44100
cases.importance: USB/headset devices often use 44.1 kHz.

cases.name: user_sample_rate_48000
cases.importance: Most OS audio stacks use 48 kHz with slightly different antialiasing behavior.

cases.name: user_bluetooth_codec_artifact
cases.importance: Bluetooth microphones are common.

cases.name: user_clipped_mic_gain_high
cases.importance: Bad gain staging should not drop commands.

cases.name: user_laptop_fan_and_speech
cases.importance: Local assistants run on laptops.

cases.name: user_far_field_across_room
cases.importance: Far-field commands must still reach callback.

cases.name: user_far_field_with_tv
cases.importance: Difficult but important living-room case.

cases.name: user_kitchen_appliance
cases.importance: Kitchen assistants are common.

cases.name: user_car_cabin_noise
cases.importance: Mobile/vehicle voice use matters.

cases.name: user_plosive_heavy_speech
cases.description: User speech with plosive mic pops.
cases.importance: Close microphones often pop on consonants.

cases.name: user_room_reverb_voice
cases.importance: Room sound affects user speech too.

cases.name: user_compressed_hard_limited
cases.importance: AGC/limiters can distort user voice.

cases.name: user_low_battery_mic_noise
cases.importance: Cheap microphones add broadband noise.

cases.name: user_open_window_traffic
cases.importance: Traffic swells should not drop commands.

cases.name: user_speaker_change_pitch_low
cases.importance: Low-pitched speakers can have lower VAD confidence.

cases.name: user_speaker_change_pitch_high
cases.importance: High-pitched speakers can be over-filtered or misclassified.

cases.name: user_fast_syllables
cases.importance: Fast talkers should be captured.

cases.name: user_slow_syllables
cases.importance: Slow talkers with pauses should not be dropped.

cases.name: user_near_speech_with_assistant_echo
cases.description: User speech while assistant echo is present.
cases.importance: Barge-in capture must preserve user speech.

cases.name: user_clipped_with_babble
cases.importance: Multiple degradations combine in practice.

cases.name: user_quiet_with_music
cases.importance: Low-volume users are easily missed.

cases.name: user_emergency_help_short
cases.importance: Safety-critical short commands must fire.

cases.name: user_wake_followup_short
cases.importance: Wakeword workflows often use short commands.

cases.name: user_noisy_usb_mic
cases.importance: USB microphones vary widely.

cases.name: user_table_reflection_comb_filter
cases.importance: Comb filtering should not defeat endpointing.

cases.name: user_phone_speaker_feedback
cases.description: User near a phone playing audio.
cases.importance: Phones frequently play background media.

cases.name: user_repeated_small_pauses
cases.description: User speech with long inter-word pauses.
cases.importance: Soft-decay should preserve natural pauses.

cases.name: echo_tablet_kickstand_reflection
cases.description: Tablet on kickstand reflecting off desk.
cases.importance: Tablets are common always-on assistant devices.

cases.name: echo_open_back_headphones_leak
cases.importance: Headset users can still leak TTS into microphones.

cases.name: echo_dual_desktop_monitors
cases.importance: Desktop geometry creates strong multi-path reflections.

cases.name: echo_smart_display_countertop
cases.importance: Smart displays combine speaker and mic in reflective kitchens.

cases.name: background_alarm_beep_sequence
cases.importance: Alarms are loud periodic bursts near microphones.

cases.name: background_phone_notification_cluster
cases.importance: Notification sounds should not start listening.

cases.name: background_mic_cable_crackle
cases.importance: Hardware noise can look like voiced transients.

cases.name: background_roommate_laugh_far
cases.importance: Non-command human vocalization should not trigger STT.

cases.name: user_whisper_like_low_energy
cases.importance: Low-energy users should still be served when close to mic.

cases.name: user_command_after_loud_noise
cases.description: Command immediately after a loud noise.
cases.importance: Users often speak right after moving objects or tapping devices.
